---
title: "simulation"
format: html
editor: visual
---

# Simulation:

```{r}

library(tidyverse)
library(lme4)
library(MASS) 

```

# Step 1: Specify data-generating function

important: cosine similarity and agreement likely to be correlated, so I add rho = 0.3 into sim between the two variables.

```{r}

simulate_agreement_and_cosine_data_shared_RE <- function(
  n_papers = 50,
  n_dims   = 9,

  # fixed effects...
  # ...for RQ1
  beta_intercept_agreement  = qlogis(0.78), # value in qlogis is human-human interrater agreement
  regcheckhuman_agreement = qlogis(0.75), # value in qlogis is mean regcheck-human agreement
  
  # ...for RQ2
  beta_intercept_cosine  = 0.82, # mean human-human cosine similarity
  regcheckhuman_cosine = 0.82, # mean regcheck-human cosine similarity
  

  # random intercepts for models 
  sd_dimension_agreement = .7, # random effect for dimension on agreement; logit scale
  sd_paper_agreement     = .7, # random effect for paper on agreement; logit scale
  sd_dimension_cosine   = 0.1, # random effect for dimension on cosine
  sd_paper_cosine       = 0.1, # random effect for paper on cosine

  # intercorrelations
  rho_row = 0.3, # correlation between agreement and cosine similarity
  sd_row_agreement = 0.7,   # defined on logit scale
  sd_row_cosine   = 0.1,  # defined on cosine scale

  # design
  number_of_raters = 2, # number of raters
  seed = NULL, 
  clip_cosine_01 = TRUE # this is to make sure cosine doesn't go outside possible values
) {
  
  # define differences for model contrast codes
  # rq1
  beta_humanregcheck_agreement <- regcheckhuman_agreement - beta_intercept_agreement
  # rq2
  beta_humanregcheck_cosine <- regcheckhuman_cosine - beta_intercept_cosine
  
  # set seed if specified
  if (!is.null(seed)) set.seed(seed)
  # stop if correlation values are impossible
  stopifnot(abs(rho_row) < 1)

  # arbitrary identifiers for papers and dimensions
  paper_ids <- paste0("P", seq_len(n_papers))
  dims      <- paste0("D", seq_len(n_dims))

  # create basic data: papers x dimensions
  base <- expand.grid(
    paper_id  = paper_ids,
    dimension = dims,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  data_humanhuman <- base |> mutate(rater_pair = "human_human")
  data_humanregcheck <- bind_rows(rep(list(base), number_of_raters)) |> # repeat the base per n human raters
    mutate(rater_pair = "human_regcheck",
           rater_id = rep(seq_len(number_of_raters), each = nrow(base))) 
  
  # create combined, labelled base data
  data <- bind_rows(data_humanhuman, data_humanregcheck)

  # ensure factors are correctly specified 
  data$rater_pair <- factor(data$rater_pair, levels = c("human_human", "human_regcheck"))
  data$dimension  <- factor(data$dimension)
  data$paper_id   <- factor(data$paper_id)

  # Shared random effect draws (raw ~ N(0,1)), then scaled per outcome
  
  # raw draws
  u_dim_raw <- rnorm(n_dims, 0, 1)
  names(u_dim_raw) <- dims
  
  v_paper_raw <- rnorm(n_papers, 0, 1)
  names(v_paper_raw) <- paper_ids

  # scale for agreement
  u_dim_agreement <- sd_dimension_agreement * u_dim_raw
  v_paper_agreement <- sd_paper_agreement * v_paper_raw

  # scale for cosine
  u_dim_cosine <- sd_dimension_cosine * u_dim_raw
  v_paper_cosine <- sd_paper_cosine * v_paper_raw
  

  # Linear predictors (without row-level noise)
  lp_agree <- beta_intercept_agreement +
    beta_humanregcheck_agreement * (data$rater_pair == "human_regcheck") +
    u_dim_agreement[as.character(data$dimension)] +
    v_paper_agreement[as.character(data$paper_id)]

  mu_cos <- beta_intercept_cosine +
    beta_humanregcheck_cosine * (data$rater_pair == "human_regcheck") +
    u_dim_cosine[as.character(data$dimension)] +
    v_paper_cosine[as.character(data$paper_id)]

  # add row-level noise from correlations
  Sigma <- matrix(
    c(sd_row_agreement^2, rho_row * sd_row_agreement * sd_row_cosine,
      rho_row * sd_row_agreement * sd_row_cosine, sd_row_cosine^2),
    nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE
  )
  
  eps <- MASS::mvrnorm(n = nrow(data), mu = c(0, 0), Sigma = Sigma)
  eps_agree <- eps[, 1]  # added on logit scale
  eps_cos   <- eps[, 2]  # added on cosine scale

  # Outcomes
  data$agreement <- rbinom(nrow(data), 1, plogis(lp_agree + eps_agree))
  data$cos_sim   <- mu_cos + eps_cos
  if (clip_cosine_01) data$cos_sim <- pmin(1, pmax(0, data$cos_sim))

  data
}

```

# Step 2: define helpers for simulation

```{r}

# Define two analyses: one for comparing agreement, one for comparing cosine
analyze_simulated_data <- function(data, 
                                   conf = 0.95, 
                                   n_draws_delta = 4000) {
  
  # initialise empty output list
  out <- list()

  # agreement fit - rq1
  fit_agreement <- suppressWarnings(tryCatch(
    glmer(agreement ~ rater_pair + (1|dimension) + (1|paper_id),
          data = data, family = binomial(),
          control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 2e5))),
    error = function(e) NULL
  ))

  # cosine fit - rq2
  fit_cosine <- suppressWarnings(tryCatch(
    lmer(cos_sim ~ rater_pair + (1|dimension) + (1|paper_id),
         data = data,
         control = lmerControl(check.conv.singular = "ignore")),
    error = function(e) NULL
  ))

  # check if fits are in order
  out$ok_agree <- !is.null(fit_agreement)
  out$ok_cos   <- !is.null(fit_cosine)

  # observed correlation (should be broadly consistent with rho_raw)
  out$obs_cor <- suppressWarnings(cor(data$agreement, data$cos_sim))

  # define z for computing CIs
  z <- qnorm(1 - (1 - conf) / 2)

  
  
  # Extract probability-scaled coefficients for RQ1 model
  if (out$ok_agree) {
    fe <- fixef(fit_agreement)
    V  <- tryCatch(vcov(fit_agreement), error = function(e) NULL)

    alpha_hat <- unname(fe["(Intercept)"]) # human_human log-odds at RE=0
    beta_hat  <- unname(fe["rater_pairhuman_regcheck"]) # human_regcheck - human_human on log-odds

    # get coefs
    out$agree_beta0_hat  <- alpha_hat
    out$agree_betahuman_regcheck_hat <- beta_hat

    # probability-scale point estimates 
    p_human_human_hat <- plogis(alpha_hat)
    p_human_regcheck_hat <- plogis(alpha_hat + beta_hat)
    out$agree_phuman_human_hat <- p_human_human_hat
    out$agree_phuman_regcheck_hat <- p_human_regcheck_hat
    out$agree_delta_hat_prob <- p_human_regcheck_hat - p_human_human_hat

    # Wald CI for beta on logit scale 
    if (!is.null(V) && all(c("(Intercept)","rater_pairhuman_regcheck") %in% rownames(V))) {
      se_beta <- sqrt(unname(V["rater_pairhuman_regcheck","rater_pairhuman_regcheck"]))
      out$agree_ci_width_betahuman_regcheck_logit <- 2 * z * se_beta
    } else {
      out$agree_ci_width_betahuman_regcheck_logit <- NA_real_
    }

    # MVN draws of (alpha, beta) -> transform to Delta on probability scale
    if (!is.null(V) && all(c("(Intercept)","rater_pairhuman_regcheck") %in% rownames(V))) {
      idx <- c("(Intercept)", "rater_pairhuman_regcheck")
      V2 <- V[idx, idx, drop = FALSE]

      draws <- MASS::mvrnorm(
        n = n_draws_delta,
        mu = c(alpha_hat, beta_hat),
        Sigma = V2
      )

      alpha <- draws[, 1]
      beta  <- draws[, 2]
      delta_prob <- plogis(alpha + beta) - plogis(alpha)

      a <- (1 - conf) / 2
      ci <- unname(quantile(delta_prob, probs = c(a, 1 - a), na.rm = TRUE))

      out$agree_delta_ci_lo_prob <- ci[1]
      out$agree_delta_ci_hi_prob <- ci[2]
      out$agree_delta_ci_width_prob <- ci[2] - ci[1]
    } else {
      out$agree_delta_ci_lo_prob <- NA_real_
      out$agree_delta_ci_hi_prob <- NA_real_
      out$agree_delta_ci_width_prob <- NA_real_
    }

  } else {
    out$agree_beta0_hat <- out$agree_betahuman_regcheck_hat <- NA_real_
    out$agree_phuman_human_hat <- out$agree_phuman_regcheck_hat <- NA_real_
    out$agree_delta_hat_prob <- NA_real_
    out$agree_delta_ci_lo_prob <- out$agree_delta_ci_hi_prob <- out$agree_delta_ci_width_prob <- NA_real_
    out$agree_ci_width_betahuman_regcheck_logit <- NA_real_
  }

  
  
  # Cosine fit RQ2 (betahuman_regcheck is already on cosine scale)
  if (out$ok_cos) {
    fe <- fixef(fit_cosine)
    V  <- tryCatch(vcov(fit_cosine), error = function(e) NULL)

    # get coefs
    out$cos_beta0_hat  <- unname(fe["(Intercept)"])
    out$cos_betahuman_regcheck_hat <- unname(fe["rater_pairhuman_regcheck"])

    if (!is.null(V) && "rater_pairhuman_regcheck" %in% rownames(V)) {
      se <- sqrt(unname(V["rater_pairhuman_regcheck","rater_pairhuman_regcheck"]))
      out$cos_ci_width_betahuman_regcheck <- 2 * z * se
    } else {
      out$cos_ci_width_betahuman_regcheck <- NA_real_
    }
  } else {
    out$cos_beta0_hat <- out$cos_betahuman_regcheck_hat <- out$cos_ci_width_betahuman_regcheck <- NA_real_
  }

  as.data.frame(out)
}




```

# Step 3: Build parameter grid

```{r}

# define helper function for building full parameter grid
make_param_grid <- function(
  beta_intercept_agreement,
  regcheckhuman_agreement,
  n_papers,
  beta_intercept_cosine,
  regcheckhuman_cosine,
  sd_dimension_agreement,
  sd_paper_agreement,
  sd_dimension_cosine,
  sd_paper_cosine,
  rho_row
) {
  expand.grid(
    beta_intercept_agreement = beta_intercept_agreement,
    regcheckhuman_agreement = regcheckhuman_agreement,
    n_papers = n_papers,
    beta_intercept_cosine = beta_intercept_cosine,
    regcheckhuman_cosine = regcheckhuman_cosine,
    sd_dimension_agreement = sd_dimension_agreement,
    sd_paper_agreement = sd_paper_agreement,
    sd_dimension_cosine = sd_dimension_cosine,
    sd_paper_cosine = sd_paper_cosine,
    rho_row = rho_row,
    KEEP.OUT.ATTRS = FALSE,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}


run_simulations <- function(
  param_grid,
  n_reps = 200,
  n_dims = 9,
  number_of_raters = 2,
  seed = 1,
  # keep nuisance fixed (change if you want)
  sd_row_agreement = 0.7,
  sd_row_cosine = 0.06,
  clip_cosine_01 = TRUE
) {
  
  # set seed
  set.seed(seed)

  # assign scenario ids
  param_grid$scenario_id <- seq_len(nrow(param_grid))

  # create object for storing output
  per_rep <- vector("list", nrow(param_grid) * n_reps)
  k <- 0

  # iterate scenarios
  for (s in seq_len(nrow(param_grid))) {
    parameter_grid <- param_grid[s, ]

    # run replications within each scenario
    for (r in seq_len(n_reps)) {
      k <- k + 1L

      # simulate dataset
      data <- simulate_agreement_and_cosine_data_shared_RE(
        n_papers = parameter_grid$n_papers,
        n_dims = n_dims,
        beta_intercept_agreement  = parameter_grid$beta_intercept_agreement,
        regcheckhuman_agreement = parameter_grid$regcheckhuman_agreement,
        beta_intercept_cosine    = parameter_grid$beta_intercept_cosine,
        regcheckhuman_cosine   = parameter_grid$regcheckhuman_cosine,
        sd_dimension_agreement = parameter_grid$sd_dimension_agreement,
        sd_paper_agreement     = parameter_grid$sd_paper_agreement,
        sd_dimension_cosine   = parameter_grid$sd_dimension_cosine,
        sd_paper_cosine       = parameter_grid$sd_paper_cosine,
        rho_row = parameter_grid$rho_row,
        sd_row_agreement = sd_row_agreement,
        sd_row_cosine   = sd_row_cosine,
        number_of_raters = number_of_raters,
        seed = NULL,
        clip_cosine_01 = clip_cosine_01
      )

      # run analysis 
      ans <- analyze_simulated_data(data, conf = 0.95, n_draws_delta = 4000)

      # store output
      per_rep[[k]] <- dplyr::bind_cols(
        tibble::tibble(scenario_id = parameter_grid$scenario_id, rep = r),
        tibble::as_tibble(parameter_grid |> dplyr::select(-scenario_id)),
        tibble::as_tibble(ans)
)
    }

    message(sprintf("Done scenario %d / %d", s, nrow(param_grid)))
  }

  # create raw simulation output of all reps and scenarios
  per_rep <- dplyr::bind_rows(per_rep)

  # Summarise per scenario
  get_summaries <- function(x) c(
    mean = mean(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    p90 = unname(quantile(x, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE)),
    p95 = unname(quantile(x, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE))
  )

  # split by scenario/param combo
  by_scenario <- split(per_rep, per_rep$scenario_id)

  # for each parameter combo, get statistics
  per_scenario <- do.call(rbind, lapply(by_scenario, function(data) {
    data.frame(
      scenario_id = data$scenario_id[1],
      n_reps = nrow(data),

      agree_fit_rate = mean(data$ok_agree),
      cos_fit_rate   = mean(data$ok_cos),

      obs_cor_mean = mean(data$obs_cor, na.rm = TRUE),
      obs_cor_p50  = median(data$obs_cor, na.rm = TRUE),

      agree_betahuman_regcheck_hat_mean = mean(
        data$agree_betahuman_regcheck_hat, na.rm = TRUE
        ),
      cos_betahuman_regcheck_hat_mean   = mean(
        data$cos_betahuman_regcheck_hat, na.rm = TRUE
        ),

      agree_delta_ci_width_prob_mean = get_summaries(
        data$agree_delta_ci_width_prob
        )["mean"],
      agree_delta_ci_width_prob_p90 = get_summaries(
        data$agree_delta_ci_width_prob
        )["p90"],
      cos_ci_width_betahuman_regcheck_mean = get_summaries(
        data$cos_ci_width_betahuman_regcheck
        )["mean"],
      cos_ci_width_betahuman_regcheck_p90 = get_summaries(
        data$cos_ci_width_betahuman_regcheck
        )["p90"],

      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }))

  # attach parameter columns once (unique per scenario)
  per_scenario <- merge(
    unique(param_grid),
    per_scenario,
    by = "scenario_id",
    all.x = TRUE
  )
  per_scenario <- per_scenario[order(per_scenario$scenario_id), ]

  list(per_rep = per_rep, per_scenario = per_scenario)
}



```

# Step 4: Make parameter grid and run simulation

```{r}

# Choose values to run (edit as needed)
param_grid <- make_param_grid(
  beta_intercept_agreement  = qlogis(c(0.75, 0.78)),
  regcheckhuman_agreement = qlogis(c(0.75, 0.78)),   # example: human_regcheck probs relative to human_human=0.78
  n_papers     = c(50, 100, 150),
  beta_intercept_cosine    = c(0.78, 0.82),
  regcheckhuman_cosine   = c(0.78, 0.82),
  sd_dimension_agreement = c(1),
  sd_paper_agreement     = c(1),
  sd_dimension_cosine   = c(0.07),
  sd_paper_cosine       = c(0.07),
  rho_row            = c(0.3, 0.5)
)

# Run the full simulation set
all_simulations <- run_simulations(
  param_grid = param_grid,
  n_reps = 10,      # increase for final planning
  n_dims = 9,
  number_of_raters = 2,
  seed = 123
)

# Results:
per_rep <- all_simulations$per_rep
per_scenario <- all_simulations$per_scenario

head(per_scenario)


```
